finding_results.notes: Inserted an on-page aftermath bridge before the command meeting: concrete custody action, direct Tina/Fenn notification handling, and Lena's embodied aftermath cost under storm pressure.
Added compact Lena-interiority beats in both flagged stretches, tied to crowd/camera pressure at the ramp and command-pressure physiology in Conference B.
Rebuilt the ending around an enacted Tina lead: Lena initiates immediate contact and moves to intercept, with explicit urgency obstacles from command routing and incoming storm/flood risk.

Revised second-victim wound language from single-mechanism throat-cut certainty to forensic-observation ambiguity (ligature groove plus secondary neck trauma), preserving horror while keeping compatibility with later confession mechanics.
Reshaped command-room dialogue cadence for idiolect separation: Clay's long framing control language, Beau's colloquial practical pushback, and Lena's procedural-to-blunt compression.
Preserved the chapter's canonical still-face beat and avoided introducing any second 'went still/went flat' facial-template construction elsewhere in chapter 3.

summary: Chapter 3 now resolves all packet findings for this pass: post-ramp causality is embodied and procedural on-page, close-third interior anchoring is restored in both drought spans, injury wording remains compatible with downstream confession logic, command-scene voices are more distinct under pressure, the single canonical still-face image is retained without duplication, and the exit beat now drives immediate Tina-focused investigative propulsion under explicit urgency.
